hero.body: Wireframes, frontend, backend, databases, DevOps, cloud infrastructure, and AI agents. I don't hand off at the column boundary. Across fintech, banking, NBFCs, commerce, and enterprise AI — I've built it all, end to end, many times over.
hero.footnote: 10+ products shipped · 10+ industries · 100k+ events/day · Data to AI

stats.label: At a glance
stats.description: Aggregate numbers from production systems — not a single project. These are the compound results of building across fintech, commerce, AI, and platform engineering over many years.

philosophy.label: Philosophy
philosophy.title: No handoffs.
paragraphs: I didn't choose a lane. I chose the whole road. Strategy, wireframes, system design, frontend, backend, databases, cloud infra, DevOps, data pipelines, AI agents, shipping, post-launch iteration — I've owned all of it, repeatedly, across many engagements.

That breadth isn't a flex. It's a working style. When one person understands the product, the data, the infrastructure, and the user all at once, things don't fall through the cracks between teams. They get built right the first time — and fixed fast when they don't.

The three spotlights on this site are examples, not the ceiling. The same depth of ownership applies whether the next build is a lending data mart, a conversion-optimized storefront, or a voice agent for a Fortune-scale brand.

breadth.label: Beyond the spotlights
breadth.title: Many products.
breadth.spotlightNote: Three deep dives below — representative of multiple builds across data, commerce, and AI. The domains shift; the ownership model and quality bar don't.

aboutPage.metaDescription: About Piyush Jha — full-stack product engineer. Fintech, AI agents, cloud infra, commerce, and everything in between.
aboutPage.label: About
aboutPage.headline: One engineer. The whole stack. No gaps.
paragraphs: I'm Piyush Jha — a full-stack product engineer who has never believed in specializing early. Over several years I've shipped 10+ products and platforms across fintech, banking NBFCs, e-commerce, voice AI, HR tech, health data, and internal tooling. I own the arc: from wireframe and system design to the last deployment config and post-launch instrumentation.

My work spans the full technical surface area — data platforms and banking integrations on one end, LLM orchestration and enterprise voice agents on the other, with React frontends, Node and Python backends, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and database engineering filling everything in between. I've built for regulated financial institutions, shipped commerce platforms with deep observability, and delivered AI calling agents for major enterprise clients.

This site highlights three representative deep dives. They are examples of a much larger catalog — patterns repeated across fintech data platforms, commerce builds, and enterprise AI. When the right collaboration appears, this is the reference point.

principles.title: Own the arc
principles.body: From ambiguous brief to shipped software — strategy, design, build, deploy, and iteration without the gaps that appear between teams. Not one hat. All of them.

principles.title: Breadth as a feature
principles.body: Understanding the product surface, the APIs and data behind it, the dashboards that expose it, and the cloud infra it runs on — simultaneously — produces better systems than any handoff chain.

principles.title: Craft over checklist
principles.body: Interfaces should feel considered. Databases should be queryable. Pipelines should fail gracefully. Voice agents should sound human. Every engagement raises the bar.

principles.title: Measure what matters
principles.body: Dashboards, funnels, call outcomes, pipeline SLAs, system reliability — instrumentation is part of the product, not a phase-two ticket.

principles.title: Repeatable excellence
principles.body: Patterns honed across 10+ production builds mean the next domain — insurance, travel, lending, retail — ships faster without cutting corners.

aboutPage.ctaDescription: Long-form case studies with metrics, architecture notes, and walkthroughs — each one example among many.

caseStudy.overviewNote: This page documents one representative build. The same approach — full ownership from brief to production — has been applied across multiple programs in this and adjacent domains.
caseStudy.loomHint: Add loomEmbedId under projects[].media in content/site.json

projects.title: Financial Data Platform
projects.subtitle: ETL, banking integrations & executive dashboards
projects.tagline: Unified data pipelines and real-time visibility for NBFCs and banking partners — from raw ingestion to decision-ready ops dashboards.
projects.overview: One of several data platforms delivered for regulated finance. This spotlight covers end-to-end ownership: heterogeneous source ingestion, quality enforcement at scale, banking API integrations, and dashboards that operations and leadership actually use for daily decisions — not vanity charts.
projects.challenge: Legacy systems, inconsistent schemas from multiple banking partners, and manual reconciliation created dangerous blind spots across lending portfolios. Leadership needed trustworthy, fresh metrics — not ad-hoc spreadsheet exports that were already stale by the time they arrived.

approach.title: Discovery & strategy
approach.description: Mapped source systems, compliance constraints, reconciliation workflows, and stakeholder decision patterns. Defined SLAs for data freshness, set ownership boundaries, and planned rollout phases across business units — before touching a schema.

approach.title: Pipeline architecture
approach.description: Built resilient ETL flows with validation gates, idempotent transforms, and observability at every stage — from raw ingestion through standardized and curated warehouse layers. Failures surfaced fast; recoveries ran automatically.

approach.title: Banking integrations
approach.description: Orchestrated secure integrations with banking APIs and partner data feeds, normalizing diverse event formats into a single analytical model without leaking domain complexity downstream.

approach.title: Dashboards & delivery
approach.description: Shipped executive and operational dashboards with drill-down paths, role-aware views, and performance tuned for daily use — the kind that replace the morning Excel export, not supplement it.

outcomes: 96% reduction in ops turnaround time for critical workflows
100% automated reconciliation and daily reporting in production
100k+ transactions handled daily across integrated sources
Five or more banks and NBFCs connected through secure integrations

projects.title: Commerce Experience
projects.subtitle: Full-stack ecommerce with observability built in
projects.tagline: A complete storefront — catalog to checkout — instrumented for growth and performance from day one.
projects.overview: Representative of multiple commerce builds — owning the full lifecycle from information architecture and UI systems through transactional backend, payments, and behavioral analytics. Every release measurable, every funnel step traceable, every performance regression visible before customers feel it.
projects.challenge: Commerce teams need velocity without flying blind. Every release should be measurable, every funnel step traceable, and every performance regression visible before customers feel it. The infrastructure has to handle real money flows with the same care as the interface handles real users.

approach.title: Product design & IA
approach.description: Wireframed journeys for discovery, cart, and checkout. Established design tokens and component patterns so marketing and engineering could ship pages without fragmenting the experience.

approach.title: Storefront engineering
approach.description: Built a fast, accessible frontend with responsive layouts, optimistic interactions, and SEO-aware rendering — optimized for conversion and long-term maintainability, not just launch speed.

approach.title: Transactional backend
approach.description: Implemented catalog, inventory, orders, and payment handoffs with clear domain boundaries, audit trails, and failure modes designed for real money flows. Edge cases thought through before they became incidents.

approach.title: Events & performance
approach.description: Integrated event tracking across the funnel and performance budgets in CI — connecting product analytics to engineering signals in one coherent narrative, not two separate dashboards.

outcomes: Peak API latency kept under 200ms during high-traffic periods
20% increase in sales after launch and optimization cycles
10k+ product and funnel events tracked daily for growth decisions
Five or more third-party integrations wired without fragmenting the stack

media.videoCaption: Storefront, funnel events & performance monitoring overview

projects.title: Conversational Intelligence
projects.subtitle: Enterprise voice agents for major brands — at scale
projects.tagline: Natural, domain-aware voice agents for banking, insurance, travel, and retail — designed to sound human, operate reliably, and never stop improving.
projects.overview: A deep dive into one vertical of many — production voice AI agents across travel, banking, insurance, and retail for multiple enterprise clients. Conversation design, LLM orchestration, telephony integration, evaluation frameworks, and delivery playbooks — at enterprise scale, not demo scale.
projects.challenge: Generic chatbots fail in regulated, high-stakes domains. Agents must handle ambiguity without freezing, stay on-brand without sounding scripted, recover gracefully from unexpected input, and remain observable and improvable in production — all while keeping call latency low enough to feel like a real conversation.

approach.title: Conversation design
approach.description: Structured flows around real user intent — pre-call context loading, phase detection, graceful fallbacks, and escalation logic — with language tuned to brand tone, not template bot voice.

approach.title: Agent architecture
approach.description: Composed LLM orchestration, tool use, retrieval augmentation, and guardrails into testable, modular pipelines. Built regression-aware evaluation harnesses for dialogue paths that matter.

approach.title: Voice & telephony
approach.description: Integrated STT/TTS pipelines and telephony infrastructure for both outbound and inbound scenarios — optimizing for latency, barge-in handling, and turn-taking that feels natural on a phone call.

approach.title: Client delivery & iteration
approach.description: Partnered with enterprise teams through POC → pilot → production, building feedback loops, analytics on call outcomes, and continuous iteration cycles that improve agents after each deployment.

outcomes: 10k+ production calls handled monthly across deployed programs
90% reduction in calls requiring human agents
Sub-500ms latency for natural phone-native conversations
Ten or more voice agents designed, built, and delivered for enterprise clients

media.videoCaption: Voice agent console, live calls & evaluation workflow
